statement: The `K`-span of the orbit `{(f^n) v | n : ℕ}` is invariant under `f`.
lean_statement:
theorem span_orbit_mem_invtSubmodule_moduleEnd
    {K V : Type*} [CommSemiring K] [AddCommMonoid V] [Module K V]
    (f : Module.End K V) (v : V) :
    (Submodule.span K (Set.range fun n : ℕ => (f ^ n) v)) ∈ f.invtSubmodule
proof:
Proof: let `W` be the submodule spanned by the orbit. To show `W` is invariant, it suffices to
check that `f` sends each generator `(f ^ n) v` back into `W`. But
`f ((f ^ n) v) = (f ^ (n + 1)) v`, which is again an element of the orbit, hence lies in the set
whose span defines `W`. By linearity, `f` therefore maps all of `W` into `W`.
